bridge.note: One noun, two genders, three meanings: Italian's feminine la fine (the end) vs masculine il finale (a finale) vs Spanish's masculine el final (both jobs). The article contraction — alla vs al — is carrying a whole gender system on its back.

interference.es_to_it: Gender bites: Italian LA fine (feminine) vs Spanish EL final (masculine) — so the contraction differs, alla vs al. And Italian il finale (masc.) means the ending of a film; la fine is the end of things.
interference.it_to_es: Italians reach for 'a la fin' — archaic outside 'al fin y al cabo'. The everyday marker is al final.

review.notes: From Joe's Italian teacher's prepositions crash course, 2026-07-16. ES pairing chosen by Claude, unverified — reviewer to confirm.
